title: China-to-Azerbaijan Solar PV Inverter Compliance Gap Matrix
provenance: AI-compiled, multi-model cross-checked, not human-verified. Informational only; confirm with the official regulator and a qualified professional. Not legal/certification/customs/market-access advice.

rows.requirement: AZSTAND Mandatory Conformity Certification — Azerbaijan National Market Access Route (NOT EAEU / NOT EAC TR CU)
rows.target_requirement: CRITICAL MARKET STRUCTURE NOTE: Azerbaijan is NOT a member of the Eurasian Economic Union (EAEU). EAC conformity marking (EAC TR CU) under the EAEU technical regulations — including TR CU 004/2011 (Low voltage equipment safety) and TR CU 020/2011 (EMC) — does NOT apply to Azerbaijan and does NOT grant Azerbaijan market access. The mandatory conformity route for electrical products in Azerbaijan is through AZSTAND (State Committee for Standardization, Metrology and Patents of Azerbaijan, also referred to as 'Azstandart'). AZSTAND operates mandatory conformity certification for regulated electrical products placed on the Azerbaijan market. PV inverters falling within AZSTAND's current mandatory certification scope require an AZSTAND certificate of conformity before import or sale. The applicant (local importer, authorized representative, or manufacturer) must engage an AZSTAND-accredited conformity assessment body, provide technical documentation and test reports based on applicable AZS national standards (GOST/IEC-derived), and mark the product accordingly. AZSTAND publishes the list of products subject to mandatory certification at azstand.gov.az. The exact mandatory scope for PV inverters must be verified with AZSTAND before shipment as the scope can change.
target_standards: AZS national standards (GOST/IEC-derived) for electrical equipment safety and EMC — adopted by AZSTAND
AZSTAND mandatory conformity certification list and procedures — azstand.gov.az
Azerbaijan Law on Technical Regulation and Technical Requirements for Products
IEC 62109-1 and IEC 62109-2 — may be referenced as the basis for AZS-adopted safety standards for PV inverters
NOTE: EAC TR CU 004/2011 and TR CU 020/2011 are EAEU regulations and do NOT apply to Azerbaijan
rows.china_equivalent: China-side PV inverter conformity commonly relies on GB/T 37408-2021, manufacturer safety testing, and IEC 62109 CB scheme reports for export. These are useful due-diligence inputs for building an AZSTAND technical documentation package but do not substitute for the mandatory Azerbaijan conformity certificate, AZSTAND-accredited-body testing, or marking obligations. Chinese exporters who have obtained EAC TR CU certificates for EAEU markets (Kazakhstan, Russia, etc.) must not assume these certificates are valid in Azerbaijan — they are not, because Azerbaijan is outside the EAEU.
china_standards: GB/T 37408-2021 — 光伏并网逆变器技术要求 (Technical Requirements for Photovoltaic Grid-Connected Inverters)
IEC 62109-1 / IEC 62109-2 — CB scheme certificates (where held by the manufacturer for export markets)
NOTE: EAC TR CU certificates held for EAEU markets are NOT valid in Azerbaijan
rows.gap: Gap: The primary gap for Chinese PV inverter exporters targeting Azerbaijan is establishing the correct domestic market-access route through AZSTAND — not assuming EAEU EAC TR CU certification applies. Key actions: (a) determine whether the specific inverter falls within AZSTAND's current mandatory certification scope by checking azstand.gov.az or contacting AZSTAND directly; (b) if in scope, identify a local importer, authorized representative, or in-country applicant to hold the AZSTAND certificate; (c) engage an AZSTAND-accredited conformity assessment body for testing against applicable AZS / GOST / IEC-derived standards; (d) prepare technical documentation in Azerbaijani or Russian language as required by AZSTAND procedures; (e) do not assume EAC TR CU certificates obtained for EAEU markets (Kazakhstan, Russia, Belarus, etc.) provide any legal basis for Azerbaijan market access — they do not. IEC 62109 CB scheme certificates and IEC test reports can support the AZSTAND conformity documentation but do not replace the AZSTAND certificate where one is required.

mandatory_status.target: Mandatory where the inverter is within AZSTAND's mandatory certification scope for regulated electrical products; exact PV inverter scope must be confirmed with AZSTAND before shipment
mandatory_status.cn: Voluntary or project-specific for GB/T 37408-2021; IEC 62109 used for international certification export evidence; EAC TR CU certificates valid only in EAEU member states, NOT in Azerbaijan

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] Azerbaijan is NOT an EAEU member — EAC TR CU certification does not grant Azerbaijan market access. Treat AZSTAND mandatory conformity certification as the primary Azerbaijan domestic market-access gate for regulated electrical products. Verify whether the inverter is in AZSTAND's current mandatory scope at azstand.gov.az and engage a local applicant and AZSTAND-accredited body before shipment. GB/T 37408 or IEC 62109 reports support the technical file but do not replace the AZSTAND certificate where one is required.

source.publisher: State Committee for Standardization, Metrology and Patents of Azerbaijan (AZSTAND)

rows.requirement: IEC 62109-1 / IEC 62109-2 Safety Type-Test Evidence — Azerbaijan AZSTAND and Project-Level Requirements
rows.target_requirement: Azerbaijan's State Committee for Standardization, Metrology and Patents (AZSTAND, also referred to as 'Azstandart') is the national standards body responsible for AZS national standards, mandatory conformity certification, and technical regulation. Azerbaijan adopts AZS national standards that are derived from or aligned with GOST and IEC standards. For PV inverter safety, the internationally recognised standard is IEC 62109-1 (Safety of power converters for use in photovoltaic power systems — Part 1: General requirements) and IEC 62109-2 (Part 2: Particular requirements for inverters). Large-scale solar project owners, engineering consultants, and international lenders (EBRD, IFC) active in Azerbaijan require IEC 62109-1/-2 type-test evidence from an ILAC-accredited or IECEE CB-scheme laboratory as part of the equipment approval package. AZSTAND mandatory certification for regulated electrical products may reference GOST or IEC-aligned AZS standards; the exact scope of mandatory certification applicable to PV inverters must be confirmed with AZSTAND directly, as the published mandatory product list and applicable technical regulations can change.
target_standards: IEC 62109-1 — Safety of power converters for use in photovoltaic power systems — Part 1: General requirements
IEC 62109-2 — Safety of power converters for use in photovoltaic power systems — Part 2: Particular requirements for inverters
AZS national standards (GOST/IEC-derived) for electrical equipment safety — adopted by AZSTAND
AZSTAND mandatory conformity certification scope for regulated electrical products
IECEE CB Scheme — international CB certificates accepted as evidence in many national certification processes
rows.china_equivalent: China's primary voluntary standard for PV grid-connected inverter safety is GB/T 37408-2021 (Technical Requirements for Photovoltaic Grid-Connected Inverters). GB/T 37408 shares conceptual safety intent with IEC 62109-1/-2 but is a separate domestic standard assessed by CNAS-accredited Chinese laboratories. It is not harmonised with IEC 62109 and is not accepted as equivalent to IEC 62109 type-test evidence by Azerbaijani project owners or international lenders. Many leading Chinese PV inverter manufacturers additionally hold IECEE CB scheme certificates under IEC 62109-1/-2 for export markets; where these certificates cover the specific model and power rating being supplied to Azerbaijan, they are a strong basis for demonstrating international safety compliance.
china_standards: GB/T 37408-2021 — 光伏并网逆变器技术要求 (Technical Requirements for Photovoltaic Grid-Connected Inverters)
IECEE CB Scheme certificates under IEC 62109-1/-2 (where obtained by the Chinese manufacturer for export markets)
rows.gap: Gap: A Chinese PV inverter holding only GB/T 37408 certification cannot satisfy IEC 62109-1/-2 requirements expected by Azerbaijani project owners, engineering consultants, or international lenders. Exporters must: (a) obtain IEC 62109-1/-2 type-test certificates from an ILAC-accredited or IECEE CB-scheme laboratory covering the specific inverter model or family being supplied; (b) verify whether the model and power rating are covered by any existing CB certificate held by the manufacturer; (c) check the current AZSTAND mandatory certification scope to determine whether Azerbaijan domestic conformity requires a separate AZSTAND certificate or whether IECEE CB scheme / IEC type-test reports can support an AZSTAND conformity application. Confirm AZSTAND current mandatory product scope at azstand.gov.az before shipment.

mandatory_status.target: Mandatory in practice for project-financed utility-scale solar projects in Azerbaijan where EBRD / IFC lender specifications apply; AZSTAND mandatory domestic conformity scope for PV inverters must be confirmed directly with AZSTAND
mandatory_status.cn: Voluntary — GB/T 37408-2021 is a recommended national standard (GB/T designation); CQC/CGC voluntary certification available; IECEE CB scheme certificates are additionally held by leading Chinese manufacturers for export

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] GB/T 37408 certification alone is not accepted as IEC 62109-1/-2 safety compliance by Azerbaijan project owners or international lenders. Plan for IEC 62109-1/-2 type-test certificates from an ILAC-accredited or IECEE CB-scheme laboratory before committing equipment to Azerbaijani projects. Confirm whether AZSTAND mandatory domestic conformity certification is required for the inverter and what documents are accepted for the application at azstand.gov.az before shipment.

source.publisher: State Committee for Standardization, Metrology and Patents of Azerbaijan (AZSTAND)

rows.requirement: EMC Evidence for PV Inverters — IEC 62920 / IEC 61000-3-2 and AZSTAND Conformity
rows.target_requirement: Azerbaijan's AZSTAND adopts AZS national standards derived from or aligned with GOST and IEC standards. For electromagnetic compatibility (EMC) of PV inverters, the internationally applicable standards are IEC 62920 (Photovoltaic power generating systems — EMC requirements and test methods for power conversion equipment) and IEC 61000-3-2 (Electromagnetic compatibility — Limits for harmonic current emissions). GOST-derived EMC standards may also be referenced under AZS national standards. Large-scale project owners and lenders in Azerbaijan require EMC compliance evidence as part of the equipment specification package. The exact AZS or GOST standard applicable for mandatory AZSTAND EMC certification of PV inverters must be confirmed with AZSTAND directly.
target_standards: IEC 62920 — Photovoltaic power generating systems — EMC requirements and test methods for power conversion equipment
IEC 61000-3-2 — Electromagnetic compatibility — Limits for harmonic current emissions (equipment with input current up to 16 A per phase)
AZS / GOST-derived EMC standards adopted by AZSTAND — exact applicable standard to be confirmed with AZSTAND
AZSTAND mandatory conformity certification scope for regulated electrical products — EMC requirements
rows.china_equivalent: Chinese PV inverter EMC compliance is primarily assessed under GB/T 37408-2021, which includes EMC test requirements aligned with China's domestic framework and conducted at CNAS-accredited Chinese laboratories. While GB/T 37408 draws on IEC 61000-series concepts, it is not harmonised with IEC 62920 and is not accepted as IEC 62920 or IEC 61000-3-2 compliance evidence by Azerbaijani project owners or international lenders. Chinese manufacturers supplying to Azerbaijani utility-scale projects should prepare IEC 62920 and IEC 61000-3-2 test reports from an ILAC-accredited laboratory in addition to any domestic GB/T 37408 certificates.
china_standards: GB/T 37408-2021 — 光伏并网逆变器技术要求 (Technical Requirements for Photovoltaic Grid-Connected Inverters) — includes EMC test requirements
rows.gap: Chinese GB/T 37408 EMC testing does not satisfy IEC 62920 and IEC 61000-3-2 requirements expected by Azerbaijani project owners and international lenders. Exporters should obtain: (a) IEC 62920 EMC type-test report from an ILAC-accredited laboratory covering the specific inverter model; (b) IEC 61000-3-2 harmonic current emission test report for the model. Because Azerbaijan's nominal grid voltage (220/380 V at 50 Hz) matches China's, retesting under different voltage conditions is generally not required — but confirm this with the project's technical adviser. Separately, confirm with AZSTAND whether mandatory EMC certification is required under domestic AZS or GOST-derived standards for the specific inverter type.

mandatory_status.target: Mandatory in practice for utility-scale project lender specifications; AZSTAND mandatory domestic EMC certification scope for PV inverters must be confirmed with AZSTAND
mandatory_status.cn: Voluntary — GB/T 37408-2021 EMC testing is part of the recommended national standard; CQC/CGC voluntary certification available but not a standalone mandatory market-access requirement for domestic China PV inverters

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] Chinese GB/T 37408 EMC certificates do not satisfy IEC 62920 and IEC 61000-3-2 requirements expected by project owners and lenders in Azerbaijan. Obtain IEC 62920 and IEC 61000-3-2 type-test reports from an ILAC-accredited laboratory for the specific inverter model before project submission. Confirm with AZSTAND whether domestic AZS or GOST-derived EMC certification is separately required for import or sale of the specific inverter type in Azerbaijan.

source.publisher: State Committee for Standardization, Metrology and Patents of Azerbaijan (AZSTAND)

rows.requirement: Azerenerji / Azerishig Grid-Connection Requirements — 50 Hz / 220 V / 380 V CIS-Legacy Grid Parameters and Anti-Islanding (IEC 62116 / IEC 61727)
rows.target_requirement: Azerbaijan's electricity grid operates at 50 Hz with a CIS-legacy nominal voltage of 220 V single-phase and 380 V three-phase — the same nominal values as China's domestic grid under GB/T 156. Azerenerji is the state-owned generation and transmission company; Azerishig (AzərEnerji subsidiary / distribution arm) operates the distribution and retail network. Grid-connected PV inverters must comply with Azerbaijan's grid-connection technical requirements, which include: (1) IEC 62116 anti-islanding test compliance — inverters must detect loss-of-mains and disconnect within the required time; (2) IEC 61727 (Photovoltaic systems — Characteristics of the utility interface) for grid interface characteristics; (3) frequency and voltage protection thresholds set to Azerishig / Azerenerji network specifications; (4) power quality and harmonic limits per applicable AZS or IEC standards; (5) project-specific grid-connection approval from Azerenerji or Azerishig before energisation. Large-scale projects (e.g., Masdar Garadagh 230 MW, ACWA Power projects) follow IEC standards and lender specifications (EBRD, IFC) regardless of whether local minimums require them. IMPORTANT: The nominal CIS 220/380 V at 50 Hz grid means re-parameterisation risk versus Chinese factory firmware is low compared to markets with different nominal voltages, but Azerbaijan grid-operator approval and project-specific protection settings remain mandatory — China domestic grid-connection approval does not transfer.
target_standards: IEC 62116 — Utility-interconnected photovoltaic inverters — Test procedure of islanding prevention measures
IEC 61727 — Photovoltaic (PV) systems — Characteristics of the utility interface
IEC 61000-3-2 — Electromagnetic compatibility — Limits for harmonic current emissions
AZS national standards (GOST/IEC-derived) adopted by AZSTAND for grid-connected PV equipment
Azerenerji / Azerishig grid-connection technical specifications and project-specific connection conditions
rows.china_equivalent: Chinese grid-connection evidence commonly includes GB/T 19964-2024 (Technical Requirements for Connecting Photovoltaic Power Station to Power System, applicable to utility-scale PV connected via 10 kV and above), NB/T 32004-2018 (Technical Specification for Photovoltaic Grid-Connected Inverters), and domestic anti-islanding and protection test results. Because Azerbaijan's nominal grid voltage (220/380 V at 50 Hz) matches China's GB/T 156 nominal values, the firmware protection parameter overlap is greater than for markets with different voltage levels. However, Chinese grid-connection documents are not accepted as Azerbaijan Azerenerji or Azerishig grid-connection evidence. Project-specific technical conditions, protection relay coordination, and commissioning documentation must be obtained through the Azerbaijani grid-operator approval process.
china_standards: GB/T 19964-2024 — 光伏发电站接入电力系统技术规定 (Technical Requirements for Connecting Photovoltaic Power Station to Power System)
GB/T 156 — 标准电压 (Standard voltages) — 220/380 V at 50 Hz
NB/T 32004-2018 — 光伏并网逆变器技术规范 (Technical Specification for Photovoltaic Grid-Connected Inverters)
GB/T 37408-2021 — 光伏并网逆变器技术要求 (Technical Requirements for Photovoltaic Grid-Connected Inverters)
rows.gap: Gap: While Azerbaijan's 220/380 V at 50 Hz nominal grid voltage matches China's, Chinese GB/T grid-connection documents do not satisfy Azerbaijan Azerenerji / Azerishig grid-connection requirements. Exporters and project developers must: (a) obtain IEC 62116 anti-islanding test report from an ILAC-accredited laboratory; (b) verify inverter compliance with IEC 61727 grid interface characteristics; (c) prepare power quality and harmonic emission evidence per applicable AZS or IEC standards; (d) obtain project-specific grid-connection technical conditions and protection relay coordination approval from Azerenerji or Azerishig; (e) for large-scale projects with EBRD or IFC financing, prepare IEC-based compliance documentation package as required by the lender's environmental and social standards and equipment specifications. Confirm the current edition of Azerenerji and Azerishig grid-connection technical specifications directly with the relevant grid operator before finalising the equipment package.

mandatory_status.target: Mandatory for grid connection under Azerenerji / Azerishig technical specifications; project-specific grid-connection approval from the grid operator is a non-negotiable gate before energisation
mandatory_status.cn: Chinese grid-connection evidence satisfies China's grid operator requirements but does not transfer to Azerbaijan Azerenerji or Azerishig grid-connection acceptance

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] Azerbaijan's CIS-legacy 220/380 V at 50 Hz grid reduces firmware re-parameterisation risk compared to markets with different voltage levels, but Chinese GB/T grid-connection documents are not accepted as Azerenerji or Azerishig grid-connection evidence. Obtain IEC 62116 anti-islanding and IEC 61727 compliance evidence and secure project-specific grid-connection technical conditions from the relevant Azerbaijani grid operator before energisation. For EBRD- or IFC-financed projects, confirm the lender's specific equipment specification requirements directly with the project's environmental and social consultant.

source.publisher: Azerenerji (Joint Stock Company, Azerbaijan State Electricity Grid)

rows.requirement: Large-Scale Solar Projects — Lender (EBRD / IFC) and Project-Owner Equipment Specifications
rows.target_requirement: Azerbaijan has attracted significant utility-scale solar investment, with landmark projects including the Masdar Garadagh Solar Park (230 MW, commissioned 2023) and ACWA Power projects. These large-scale projects are typically financed by international lenders such as the European Bank for Reconstruction and Development (EBRD) and the International Finance Corporation (IFC), or structured as government-guaranteed IPP contracts. Lender and project-owner equipment specifications for such projects require IEC-based compliance documentation regardless of whether local AZSTAND minimum requirements would be sufficient. Typical lender requirements include: (a) IEC 62109-1/-2 safety type-test certificates from an IECEE CB-scheme body or equivalent ILAC-accredited laboratory; (b) IEC 62116 anti-islanding evidence; (c) grid fault ride-through (FRT) capability per IEC or project-specific specification; (d) IEC 61000-series power-quality compliance; (e) performance guarantees and data-sheet compliance per IEC or EN standards. Chinese inverters supplied to such projects must meet the project's equipment specification, not only the minimum Azerbaijan domestic standard.
target_standards: IEC 62109-1 — Safety of power converters for use in photovoltaic power systems — Part 1: General requirements
IEC 62109-2 — Safety of power converters for use in photovoltaic power systems — Part 2: Particular requirements for inverters
IEC 62116 — Utility-interconnected photovoltaic inverters — Test procedure of islanding prevention measures
IEC 61000-series — Electromagnetic compatibility and power quality
EBRD and IFC environmental and social standards — equipment technical specifications for financed renewable energy projects
Project-specific equipment specification (EPC contract or owner's engineer specification)
rows.china_equivalent: Chinese PV inverter export documentation commonly includes GB/T 37408-2021, NB/T 32004-2018, manufacturer's test reports, and in many cases IEC 62109 CB scheme certificates obtained specifically for international export markets. For Chinese inverters already holding IECEE CB scheme certificates under IEC 62109-1/-2, these certificates are a strong starting point for demonstrating lender-required safety compliance. However, additional project-specific evidence (FRT, grid settings validation, performance data, O&M documentation) will be required by the project owner or lender's technical adviser regardless of the CB certificate status.
china_standards: GB/T 37408-2021 — 光伏并网逆变器技术要求 (Technical Requirements for Photovoltaic Grid-Connected Inverters)
NB/T 32004-2018 — 光伏并网逆变器技术规范 (Technical Specification for Photovoltaic Grid-Connected Inverters)
IECEE CB Scheme certificates under IEC 62109-1/-2 (where the Chinese manufacturer has obtained these for export)
rows.gap: Gap: Chinese domestic GB/T certifications alone are insufficient for lender-financed large-scale solar projects in Azerbaijan. Exporters and project developers must confirm the project owner's or lender's technical adviser's specific equipment specification requirements before committing to a compliance package. Key actions: (a) obtain IEC 62109-1/-2 IECEE CB scheme or equivalent ILAC-accredited certificates if not already held; (b) prepare grid FRT evidence per IEC or project specification; (c) validate firmware settings for Azerbaijan 50 Hz / 220/380 V grid and document the configuration; (d) prepare IEC 61000-series power quality compliance evidence; (e) confirm with the project's owner's engineer or lender adviser which specific IEC standard editions and test conditions are required. Do not assume that meeting AZSTAND minimum domestic requirements satisfies lender specifications for EBRD- or IFC-financed projects.

mandatory_status.target: Mandatory in practice for EBRD- and IFC-financed large-scale solar projects in Azerbaijan, regardless of local AZSTAND minimum requirements; project-owner specification is the operative compliance gate
mandatory_status.cn: Chinese GB/T certifications satisfy China domestic requirements but are not substitutes for IEC-based lender specifications required for international project finance

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] For EBRD- or IFC-financed solar projects in Azerbaijan, IEC-based compliance documentation is effectively mandatory regardless of local AZSTAND minimum requirements. Confirm the project-specific equipment specification with the lender's technical adviser or owner's engineer before committing to a compliance test programme. Do not rely on AZSTAND domestic conformity alone as a substitute for lender specification compliance.

source.publisher: European Bank for Reconstruction and Development (EBRD)
